nativeRange.habitat: Coral reefs and adjacent rubble/sand zones at depths of roughly 1–25 m; adults often seen in pairs over open substrate near reef edges.

decorPreferences: live rock with caves and crevices

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding on small fishes and benthic invertebrates in the wild. In captivity it tends to accept mysis shrimp, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, chopped seafood, and other meaty frozen preparations; generally advised to offer meaty foods 2–3 times daily, especially while juveniles are settling in.

summary: A hardy Indo-Pacific spinecheek bream with a bold white-and-yellow body and two dark facial stripes; an active sand-sifting predator best suited to large fish-only or FOWLR aquariums.
careNotes: Adults are most often described as peaceful sand-stirring fish that sift mouthfuls of substrate hunting for crustaceans and worms, but they are also opportunistic predators and have been known to consume small fish and motile invertebrates that fit in their mouth — for that reason they are generally considered not reef-safe with delicate inverts. A deep sand bed and open swimming areas are typically recommended, alongside rockwork with caves for retreat. Sources disagree noticeably on minimum tank size (Reeflings cites ~200 L, LiveAquaria and Quality Marine cite ~380 L / 100 gal, other hobby write-ups suggest 180 gal / ~680 L, and FishBase-derived public-aquarium guidance is much higher); the value here biases toward the larger end given the species' adult length, active swimming, and territorial single-specimen requirement. Juveniles show a strikingly different color pattern with bold yellow-and-black stripes and are believed to act as Batesian mimics of the venomous fang blenny Meiacanthus grammistes; juvenile coloration fades as the fish matures. Reported to jump, so a tight-fitting lid is generally advised. Best kept singly — conspecifics are typically intolerant of each other in home-aquarium volumes. Sources cross-checked: LiveAquaria (100 gal, peaceful, not reef-safe), FishBase (25 cm TL, protogynous hermaphrodite, 25.1–29.3 °C preferred), Reeflings (200 L, semi-aggressive, omnivore-leaning), Quality Marine (100 gal, aggressive/solitary, may jump). Temperature averaged across sources to 24–28 °C; tank-size choice 400 L picks a common aquarium size between the retailer 380 L floor and the 680 L upper-hobby recommendation.
breedingNotes: Reported as a protogynous (female-first) sequential hermaphrodite that spawns pelagically in the wild during warmer months. Captive breeding is generally considered impractical in home aquariums.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary retailer care sheet. The catalog landing page given in the manifest (collections/miscellaneous-fish) did not list this species; the species-specific product page was located via site search and used as the primary.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy, max length (25 cm TL), depth range, distribution, preferred temperature (25.1–29.3 °C), trophic position, and protogynous reproductive mode.

additional.name: Reeflings
additional.notes: Cross-checked tank size (200 L cited), temperature (24–28 °C), pH (8.0–8.4), salinity (SG 1.020–1.025), temperament described as semi-aggressive.

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Cross-checked min tank size (100 gal / 379 L), temperament (aggressive/solitary), reef-safe status (no), max size (up to 10 in), jumping risk, and feeding cadence.

fish.breedingNotes: Pelagic broadcast spawning in the wild during warmer months; not known to be bred in home aquariums.
